rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF LAMONT ALLEN
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petiion For Authority To Control Conservatee'S Salary Under Probate Code Section 2601 And For Authority To Pay Conservatee An Allowance For Personal Use Pursuant To Probate Code Section 2421
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 8/8/2023. The following issues to be supplemented two weeks prior to the hearing: (1) Allegation of the powers granted to the conservator upon the appointment with a photocopy of applicable order. (2) Further allegation regarding why the need of controlling the conservatee's salaries became apparent and how, if any, the current situation in which the conservatee controls his own salaries affects the conservator of the estate in exercising her given powers for the conservatee. Petition alleges the conservatorship of estate has sufficient funds to continue to pay the conservatee's expenses in Para.12. (3) An authority under which Conservator requests the authority to control the conservatee's financial resources. PC 2601 appears to support otherwise. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF NORRIS ROHDE
rulings.Calendar Matter: PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 8/22/2023. PETITION TO DISTRIBUTE NET PROCEEDS: (1) Need explanation why the court needs to grant this petition prior to petition for final distribution. (2) Petition must be verified by Administrator who is the court appointed fiduciary with the duty to make the report, not by the fiduciary's attorney, whether the fiduciary is absent in this County. PC 1021(b)(2). Petitioner's attorney continues to verify documents; however, a petition is not properly before the court until the fiduciary's verification is filed unless petition presents an authority under which the court should allow the fiduciary's attorney's verification. PC 1023. (3) Supplement filed 6/21/2023 also needs to be verified by the fiduciary. (4) A certified copy of Letters from the Washington County, Texas Court. It needs to be filed conventionally pursuant to LRSF 14.59.C. (5) Notice of Directions for Remote Appearances and Access to be given to the persons entitled to. LRSF 14.1.D.6) PETITION FOR APPROVAL OF STATUS OF ADMINISTRATION: (1) Petition must be verified for the above-mentioned reason. (2) Proposed Order sets forth that the estate is now closed. However, Personal Representative has not petition a final account and petition for an order for final distribution of the estate. PC 10951. A statutorily compliant and properly verified account/report must be filed to close the estate. This Status Report does not make statutorily required allegations. (3) Notice of Directions for Remote Appearances and Access to be given to the persons entitled to. LRSF 14.1.D.6) Appears the estate is ready to close. If a VERIFIED petition for final distribution is filed two weeks prior in lieu of both petitions, both petitions can be taken off calendar. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF NORRIS ROHDE
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition To Distribute Net Proceeds From Sales Of Real Property To Sister State Personal Representative
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 8/22/2023. PETITION TO DISTRIBUTE NET PROCEEDS: (1) Need explanation why the court needs to grant this petition prior to petition for final distribution. (2) Petition must be verified by Administrator who is the court appointed fiduciary with the duty to make the report, not by the fiduciary's attorney, whether the fiduciary is absent in this County. PC 1021(b)(2). Petitioner's attorney continues to verify documents; however, a petition is not properly before the court until the fiduciary's verification is filed unless petition presents an authority under which the court should allow the fiduciary's attorney's verification. PC 1023. (3) Supplement filed 6/21/2023 also needs to be verified by the fiduciary. (4) A certified copy of Letters from the Washington County, Texas Court. It needs to be filed conventionally pursuant to LRSF 14.59.C. (5) Notice of Directions for Remote Appearances and Access to be given to the persons entitled to. LRSF 14.1.D.6) PETITION FOR APPROVAL OF STATUS OF ADMINISTRATION: (1) Petition must be verified for the above-mentioned reason. (2) Proposed Order sets forth that the estate is now closed. However, Personal Representative has not petition a final account and petition for an order for final distribution of the estate. PC 10951. A statutorily compliant and properly verified account/report must be filed to close the estate. This Status Report does not make statutorily required allegations. (3) Notice of Directions for Remote Appearances and Access to be given to the persons entitled to. LRSF 14.1.D.6) Appears the estate is ready to close. If a VERIFIED petition for final distribution is filed two weeks prior in lieu of both petitions, both petitions can be taken off calendar. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION TO TERMINATE ESTATE (NO ASSETS)
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing. NB: Except for exhibits, each paper filed with the court must bear a footer in the bottom margin of each page, placed below the page number and divided from the rest of the document page by a printed line. [Probate Code Section 1201.]

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JILL FRANCES GRIFFIN
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 7/18/23. (No earlier availability.) 1) Copies of unfiled papers were received by the examiner on 6/15/23, after the deadline posted in the tentative ruling for the hearing on 4/19/23. 2) If the decedent's father died on 9/22/22, he post-deceased the decedent. Who is his successor-in-interest? Did he have a trust? Has a personal representative been appointed for his estate? 3) The petition still has not been amended to reflect that the will is NOT lost. 4) A proof of service filed on 6/12/23 shows notice sent to numerous individuals. Nothing has been filed (no verified pleading) to indicate who they are, what their relationships to the decedent are, and to set forth their addresses. Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned examiner by 7/7/23. If there are other issues, after conformed copies of filed papers are received and reviewed by the examiner, the hearing may need to be continued again. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ERIC HARRY SASSO
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 7/26/23. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) The will waives bond only for the executor or co-executor nominated in the will. (2) It is unclear how Steven Sasso became the successor trustee of the Eric Sasso Separate Property Trust. The trust names the same fiduciaries to serve as successor trustee as those nominated to serve as executor or co-executor. Steven Sasso was the second nominated successor trustee to serve after Philip M. Sasso, and only during Settlor's incapacity, not after Settlor's death. (3) Decedent presumably left no Exhibit B to his trust, designating how tangible personal property should be distributed. If he did, any beneficiary of such tangible personal property who is not one of the beneficiaries of the residue must be identified and must receive notice. Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by 7/17/23. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF TAYA DUBONNET
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
rulings.Rulings: Hearing for the sole purpose of admitting the will. No appearance required. Status on close of estate is set for August 6, 2024.
